title: The One With The Birth Mother

jokes.text: And so incorrect.
jokes.setup: Someone says 'When you come back, you'll have a baby'
jokes.punchline: Bluntly correcting the misconception in a dry manner
jokes.explanation: Monica's characteristic precision and need to correct people, delivered with perfect economy

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: When you come back, you'll have a baby.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: When WE come back, WE'LL have a baby.

jokes.text: I could develop a condition in which I talk and talk and no one hears a word.
jokes.setup: Someone asks what they'll name the baby while she's listing concerns
jokes.punchline: Self-aware comment about being ignored mid-ramble
jokes.explanation: Great self-referential humor about Monica's tendency to overthink and ramble

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, we haven't thought about names yet. I mean, there's so much to think about - the nursery, the equipment, whether we should use cloth diapers or disposable, the environmental impact, the cost, finding the right pediatrician, schools...

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What about naming him Crap-Weasel?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: ...and we need to baby-proof the apartment, get outlet covers, cabinet locks, safety gates...

jokes.text: So we're standing firm on 'not getting our hopes up'?
jokes.setup: Monica just exclaimed 'Oh my God. She's gonna pick us!' after saying they're not getting hopes up
jokes.punchline: Sardonic observation of her immediate contradiction
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler sarcasm pointing out Monica's emotional contradiction

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, no hopes up whatsoever. I can see it in your face.

jokes.text: All right, I'll see you suckers. I'm gonna go get me a baby!
jokes.setup: Monica has been trying to stay realistic about adoption chances
jokes.punchline: Complete emotional flip to overconfident swagger
jokes.explanation: Monica's emotional swing from cautious to cocky, with funny casual phrasing

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I know the odds are against us, we might not get a baby, I need to be realistic and prepared for disappointment.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: But you know what? We're going to get a baby. We're going to get the most beautiful baby ever. Our baby is going to be perfect!

jokes.text: Screw it. I'm gonna be a daddy!
jokes.setup: Chandler was the voice of reason about not getting hopes up
jokes.punchline: Immediately caves to the excitement after Monica's outburst
jokes.explanation: Nice payoff to Chandler getting swept up in Monica's enthusiasm despite being the rational one

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Okay, okay, let's not get our hopes up. We don't even know if this is going to work out.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Are you kidding?! This is going to be amazing! We're finally going to have a baby!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh my God, we're having a baby! A baby, Monica! Our baby!

jokes.text: I wanna say, 'Someone I'm gonna have sex with.'
jokes.setup: Asked who Phoebe is with
jokes.punchline: Inappropriately sexual response that reveals his mindset
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey horndog mentality delivered with his characteristic bluntness

jokes.text: Mandy, huh? Really hot blond, big boobs?
jokes.setup: Rachel says he dated and forgot Mandy
jokes.punchline: Proves he doesn't remember by guessing generic attractive features
jokes.explanation: Joey's shallow memory of women he's dated, reinforcing his character flaws

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: You dated Mandy and forgot about her?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Mandy? Uh... yeah, sure, I remember Mandy. Blonde, right? Great smile?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: She had red hair and a limp.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh... right, right, the red hair. I knew that.

jokes.text: Might be why I don't remember, huh?
jokes.setup: Rachel says 'No' to his description of Mandy
jokes.punchline: Implies he only remembers women for their physical attributes
jokes.explanation: Reinforces Joey's shallowness but feels a bit predictable and crude

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What? She had a really great personality!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Joey, you just described her entire personality as 'really great.'

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, and?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: And you couldn't even remember her last name!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: That's not true! It was... uh... started with a 'M'?

jokes.text: Do you think I'm someone else?
jokes.setup: Joey clearly doesn't remember her friend Mandy at all
jokes.punchline: Dry response to his obvious lack of recognition
jokes.explanation: Good straight-woman response to Joey's obviousness

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I'm sorry, who are you?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: It's Mandy. We met at your apartment last week.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh right, right... Mandy. Yeah, of course!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: No, you have no idea who I am.

jokes.text: And for the one week that we went out... he didn't sleep with anybody else.
jokes.setup: Rachel praising Joey's growth and maturity during their relationship
jokes.punchline: Reveals the ridiculously low bar for his 'mature' behavior
jokes.explanation: Great setup/payoff structure with perfectly timed reveal that subverts expectations

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: You know, I have to say, you've really grown up. You're so much more mature now.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Really? You think so?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Absolutely. Like, you didn't even try to hit on my sister when she was here last week.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Well, yeah...

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: And you remembered to use a coaster! On the first try!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Thanks, Rach.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I mean, you only scratched one table this month. That's a personal record.

jokes.text: Growth.
jokes.setup: Rachel just revealed his 'mature' behavior was not cheating for one whole week
jokes.punchline: Single word proudly claiming this as personal development
jokes.explanation: Perfect comic economy - one word that encapsulates Joey's delusional self-perception

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Week!

jokes.text: Sarah! Sarah.
jokes.setup: Rachel corrects him that it's Sarah, not Mandy
jokes.punchline: Has to repeat the name to remember it
jokes.explanation: Simple callback to Joey's forgetfulness, but not particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: It's Sarah, not Mandy.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Sarah... Sarah... Sarah...

jokes.text: Well, it's-- It's a little low. Pick it up a little.
jokes.setup: Ross shows them how a hat looks on him
jokes.punchline: Monica pretends to give fashion advice while actually telling him to take it off
jokes.explanation: Nice visual setup where we imagine Monica guiding him to remove the terrible hat

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh, that's a great look for you! Very slimming. You know, it really brings out your eyes. Have you considered... not wearing it?

jokes.text: Now throw it away.
jokes.setup: Monica was giving 'adjustment' advice for Ross's hat position
jokes.punchline: Reveals her real opinion - the hat should be discarded entirely
jokes.explanation: Perfect punchline delivery after the misdirection setup - great structure and timing

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Actually, you know what? I think the hat looks great on you.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I mean, really great. Very... you.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: But if I'm being completely honest? Throw it away. Burn it. Never wear it again.

jokes.text: Now I don't know who's running for president or who that NATO guy is... but I do know that you have to get far away from that hat.
jokes.setup: Rachel claims authority from reading 30 fashion magazines a month
jokes.punchline: Admits comical ignorance of current events but total certainty about hat badness
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Rachel's priorities and the absurdity of fashion obsession

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I read 30 fashion magazines a month, so I think I know a little bit about fashion.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I have no idea what's going on in the world, but I know that hat is bad.

jokes.text: You're wearing the same shirt.
jokes.setup: Monica mocks Ross's shirt choice
jokes.punchline: Rachel points out Monica is wearing identical shirt
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor that deflates Monica's fashion criticism

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That shirt is ridiculous.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Says the woman wearing the exact same shirt.

jokes.text: Stupid Gap on every corner!
jokes.setup: Monica is embarrassed about wearing same shirt as Ross
jokes.punchline: Blames the ubiquity of Gap stores for the coincidence
jokes.explanation: Relatable complaint about chain store ubiquity, good character embarrassment

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I can't believe we're wearing the same shirt! This is so embarrassing.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: It's not my fault though—there's a Gap on every corner! What am I supposed to do?

jokes.text: It's like, 'Hi, I'm Chandler. May I have the human growing inside you?'
jokes.setup: Chandler explains why the adoption meeting feels weird
jokes.punchline: Reduces the profound experience to awkward transactional language
jokes.explanation: Great Chandler-style anxiety humor that captures the surreal nature of adoption meetings

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: It's like we're buying a car. 'Do you have any questions about the baby? Would you like to take it for a test drive?'

jokes.text: Well, obviously.
jokes.setup: Monica reassures him he'll be great at the meeting
jokes.punchline: Mock arrogant response covering his nervousness
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler defense mechanism - false confidence to mask anxiety

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You're right, you're right. I'm gonna walk in there, I'm gonna be so charming, so witty, so incredibly handsome that they'll forget all about my complete lack of qualifications.

jokes.text: That's what our friends call us.
jokes.setup: Erica mentions they only know them by file number CRW33815-D
jokes.punchline: Pretends this dehumanizing code is their actual nickname
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor with Chandler's typical deflection through nonsense

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: CRW33815-D? That's a great name! We could call you 'CRW' for short.

jokes.text: Let her finish, doctor.
jokes.setup: Erica thinks Monica is a doctor and Chandler is a reverend, Monica tries to correct
jokes.punchline: Chandler stops the correction to maintain the beneficial deception
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Chandler embracing the deception while Monica wants honesty

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No, I'm not a doctor. Chandler's not a reverend either.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa. Let her think what she wants to think.

jokes.text: Sarah reaches over and takes some fries, then...
jokes.setup: Joey building up to explain why he won't call Sarah back after their great date
jokes.punchline: The 'terrible' thing was just taking some fries
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that sets up Joey's food obsession perfectly - great character-based humor

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I can't call her back.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Something terrible happened.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I took some of her fries.

jokes.text: That's a good way to lose some fingers.
jokes.setup: Joey explaining why taking food from someone else's plate is wrong
jokes.punchline: Threatens physical violence over food theft
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Joey's food possessiveness to absurd threat level

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You don't take food off someone else's plate! That's like... that's like the rules of being around people!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You touch my food, you lose a hand.

jokes.text: Oh, no!
jokes.setup: Rachel starts to tell Phoebe about Joey's date where the girl took fries
jokes.punchline: Phoebe immediately recognizes this as a serious transgression
jokes.explanation: Good reaction showing Phoebe knows Joey well enough to understand the gravity

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: So Joey went on a date last night, and the girl took some of his fries.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh my God, she took his fries? That's like, a huge red flag!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: You don't just take someone's fries without asking. That means she has no respect for boundaries.

jokes.text: You wouldn't let her have a grape? Not me. Emma.
jokes.setup: Ross tells story of Joey not sharing grapes at breakfast with 'her'
jokes.punchline: Reveals the 'her' was baby Emma, making it even more ridiculous
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation revealing Joey's food hoarding extends even to babies - great structure

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: And you know what the worst part was? He wouldn't even share his grapes with her.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Who is 'her'?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Emma.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: He wouldn't share his grapes with a baby?

jokes.text: Joey doesn't share food!
jokes.setup: Everyone discussing his food possessiveness
jokes.punchline: Joey states his rule with pride like it's a life philosophy
jokes.explanation: Iconic Friends catchphrase that perfectly encapsulates Joey's character - became cultural reference

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey! I have a rule: you don't touch Joey's food!

jokes.text: And some mozzarella sticks. What were we talking about?
jokes.setup: Joey listing extra food to order as 'sharing buffer' but getting carried away
jokes.punchline: Gets so lost in food fantasy he forgets the original conversation
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Joey's single-minded focus on food

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Okay, so we'll get the pizza, and then maybe some wings as a sharing buffer, you know, in case someone wants something different. And garlic knots—those are important. Oh! And we should get a salad so we feel like we're eating healthy. And breadsticks, because breadsticks are—they're their own thing, you know? And maybe some dessert, like tiramisu or—or chocolate lava cake, or both, because they're different experiences. And some appetizers to start, like mozzarella sticks and—

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Joey, what are we even talking about?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What do you mean? The sharing buffer!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: We haven't even ordered yet!

jokes.text: Okay, nothing with hair. And nothing with padlocks on it!
jokes.setup: Ross rejecting various trendy clothing items at the store
jokes.punchline: Increasingly bizarre clothing features he won't wear
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy imagining ridiculous fashion items Ross is horrified by

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: No, no, no. I'm not wearing parachute pants. I'm not wearing a backwards baseball cap. And I'm definitely not wearing a wallet chain.

dialogue.speaker: Store Clerk
dialogue.line: Sir, those are all from 1995.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Exactly! And I'm also not wearing a holographic fanny pack, light-up sneakers, or a shirt made entirely of metallic fabric.

dialogue.speaker: Store Clerk
dialogue.line: Those are from different decades...

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I don't care! I'm also rejecting anything with a built-in kazoo, pants that smell like birthday cake, or shoes that honk when you walk.

dialogue.speaker: Store Clerk
dialogue.line: We don't sell any of those things.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, that's lucky for you, because I wouldn't wear them anyway!

jokes.text: Both logic and math are taking a serious hit today.
jokes.setup: Rachel claims $350 sweater marked down from $700 saves $200
jokes.punchline: Ross dryly noting the flawed reasoning in fancy language
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross line - intellectually superior tone applied to retail math absurdity

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: It was originally $700, and it's marked down to $350, so I'm saving $200!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, actually, if it's half off, you're saving $350, not $200.

jokes.text: Boys will be boys
jokes.setup: Ross trying on trendy clothing that he initially likes
jokes.punchline: Discovers shirt has this trite slogan, changing his opinion
jokes.explanation: Good setup showing Ross's standards being compromised by a cheesy slogan

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Oh, this is actually pretty cool. I like this shirt.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Wait... 'Live, Laugh, Love'? Oh no. No, no, no. I'm taking this off.

jokes.text: So you're saying... if I wear these pants, I might be getting into hers?
jokes.setup: Rachel says the right clothes will make his date go nuts for him
jokes.punchline: Awkward sexual pun about 'getting into' pants
jokes.explanation: Predictable double entendre that feels forced - Ross trying too hard to be smooth

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: The right clothes will make your date go nuts for you.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What do you mean? Like, what kind of clothes?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: You know, something that shows you've got style. Maybe she'll want to get into your pants.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I... uh... that's not really how it works for me.

jokes.text: Why do men keep talking to me like this?
jokes.setup: Ross makes crude pants pun
jokes.punchline: Rachel's exasperated response suggesting this happens frequently
jokes.explanation: Good straight-woman response that acknowledges the pattern of male behavior

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, Ross. Not the pants jokes again!

jokes.text: It says, 'Do it.'
jokes.setup: Erica asks if the Bible says anything about adoption
jokes.punchline: Monica makes up casual Biblical endorsement
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist fake reverend moment with Monica's desperate improvisation

dialogue.speaker: Erica
dialogue.line: Does the Bible say anything about adoption?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh yeah, absolutely. It says, uh... 'Adoption is good.' It's right there in Confusions.

jokes.text: And behold, she did adopt unto them a baby. And it was good.
jokes.setup: Monica already made up a Bible quote about adoption
jokes.punchline: Doubles down with elaborate Biblical language ending in casual assessment
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with authentic-sounding Biblical language undermined by casual ending

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Genesis 3:16, 'And lo, the child shall be adopted into the bosom of a loving family, and that family shall be blessed beyond measure.' It's beautiful.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Also, it's probably fake.

jokes.text: Good flock. Flock, flock, flock.
jokes.setup: Monica explaining her reverend duties and mentioning her 'flock'
jokes.punchline: Nervously repeating 'flock' like she's not sure what it means
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Monica's panic as she gets deeper into the deception

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: So I've been taking my duties as a reverend very seriously. I really care about my flock. My flock. I mean, I have a flock. A flock of... people. My flock.

jokes.text: You are so going to heaven!
jokes.setup: Erica chooses them for adoption
jokes.punchline: Monica's grateful response accidentally maintains reverend character
jokes.explanation: Good ironic moment where Monica's excitement accidentally reinforces her fake persona

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Thank you so much for choosing us. We promise to love and cherish this baby with all our hearts.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: May God bless you and keep you. The Lord is my shepherd.

jokes.text: Wait, are we in Joey's imagination?
jokes.setup: Rachel suggests trying on bras for Monica to evaluate
jokes.punchline: Monica questions if this is too good to be true for a male character
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke acknowledging Joey's fantasies and the show's own tropes

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Come on, try on some bras. I'll tell you which ones look good.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Wait, is this a dream? Am I actually going to wake up and find out this was all in my head?

jokes.text: I think we're not wearing the same shirt anymore.
jokes.setup: Chandler sees Ross in his new trendy outfit
jokes.punchline: References earlier matching shirt embarrassment, noting they've gone opposite directions
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to earlier embarrassment with understated observation

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Wow, Ross! Nice outfit. Very... trendy.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Thanks! I'm trying to update my look.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, remember when we wore matching shirts that one time? That was awkward.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: But I gotta say, we've definitely gone in opposite directions now.

jokes.text: Looks like someone is the ladies!
jokes.setup: Ross claims Chandler is jealous of his success with ladies
jokes.punchline: Chandler suggests Ross looks like a woman in his new outfit
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay insult in Chandler's style, though not particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You're just jealous because I'm successful with women.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh yeah? Well, you look like a woman in that outfit.

jokes.text: God works in mysterious ways.
jokes.setup: Monica says the mistaken identity is wrong
jokes.punchline: Chandler invokes religious justification for their deception
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Chandler's willingness to rationalize for baby

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That's not right. We can't keep lying to her.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Why not? The Bible says 'Thou shalt not bear false witness,' but it doesn't say anything about bearing false identity.

jokes.text: Your veal chop is 34.95!
jokes.setup: Monica claims she serves God by feeding the hungry and poor as a chef
jokes.punchline: Chandler points out her expensive restaurant prices
jokes.explanation: Great reality check that punctures Monica's self-serving rationalization with specific detail

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I serve God by feeding the hungry and poor as a chef.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, at thirty-eight dollars a plate.

jokes.text: Technicality!
jokes.setup: Monica points out he's Jewish so he can't be a reverend
jokes.punchline: Chandler dismisses his entire religious identity as minor detail
jokes.explanation: Good one-word dismissal showing Chandler's desperation to make adoption work

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You're Jewish, you can't be a reverend.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh, that's just a technicality.

jokes.text: Yes, they are my stuffed clams.
jokes.setup: Sarah admires the stuffed clams on Joey's plate
jokes.punchline: Joey's possessive response emphasizing ownership despite offering to share
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Joey's food possessiveness despite his attempts to be generous

dialogue.speaker: Sarah
dialogue.line: Those stuffed clams look amazing.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, they're pretty great. You want some?

dialogue.speaker: Sarah
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No! These are MY clams!

jokes.text: What? What is the matter with you? I don't like it when people take food off of my plate, okay?
jokes.setup: Sarah takes a clam after Joey said sharing was fine
jokes.punchline: Joey's explosive reaction reveals his true feelings despite earlier promises
jokes.explanation: Good payoff showing Joey can't overcome his food obsession even when trying to change

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No, no, no, no! What are you doing?! That's my clam!

dialogue.speaker: Sarah
dialogue.line: But you said we could share.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I lied! I lied, okay?! I don't share food! I don't share anything!

jokes.text: Yes, that's a raspberry coulis.
jokes.setup: Joey complains about 'red crap' on his cheesecake
jokes.punchline: Waiter's professional response to Joey's crude description of fancy garnish
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Joey's unsophisticated palate and fancy restaurant terminology

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What is this red crap?

dialogue.speaker: Waiter
dialogue.line: That's the garnish, sir.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Well, I don't want it on my plate.

dialogue.speaker: Waiter
dialogue.line: Very good, sir.

jokes.text: I'm so stupid ordering cheesecake, trying to be healthy.
jokes.setup: Joey ordered cheesecake and is disappointed by healthy fruit topping
jokes.punchline: Reveals he thought cheesecake was a health food choice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey logic - cheesecake as health food shows his relationship with nutrition

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. You're telling me cheesecake isn't healthy? I thought I was making a good choice!

jokes.text: This is a two-way street.
jokes.setup: Joey won't let Sarah have his dessert after she took his clams
jokes.punchline: Treats food sharing like a legal negotiation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Joey treating food rules like formal agreements

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You took my clams! My clams, Sarah!

dialogue.speaker: Sarah
dialogue.line: Joey, come on, just let me have a bite of your dessert.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No way! You know the rules. You take my food, you lose dessert privileges. That's in the contract.

dialogue.speaker: Sarah
dialogue.line: What contract? We never signed anything!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Exactly! Which means you're in breach. My lawyer will be calling.

jokes.text: Stop staring at me!
jokes.setup: Joey left alone with Sarah's chocolate torte after saying she can't have it
jokes.punchline: Talking to the dessert as if it's tempting him
jokes.explanation: Great visual comedy imagining Joey's internal struggle with the dessert as adversary

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I can't eat this. Sarah said I can't eat this.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You're so chocolatey. You're so moist.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Why are you doing this to me?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I have no willpower. You know I have no willpower.

jokes.text: I'm not even sorry.
jokes.setup: Joey has clearly eaten Sarah's dessert while she was away
jokes.punchline: Defiant admission of guilt without remorse
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - shameless about his food obsession trumping good manners

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I ate your dessert. It was delicious. I regret nothing.

jokes.text: I could perform an operation on you and prove it if you'd like.
jokes.setup: Chandler admitting he's not actually a doctor
jokes.punchline: Offers to prove incompetence through malpractice
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor with Chandler's typical nervous deflection through absurdity

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm not actually a doctor.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You want me to prove it? I can perform some malpractice for you.

jokes.text: See? I could be a reverend.
jokes.setup: Monica makes religious reference about 'bearing false witness'
jokes.punchline: Chandler claims this proves his pastoral potential
jokes.explanation: Decent callback showing Chandler's continued delusion about fake identity

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You can't bear false witness.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: See? This is exactly why I should be a pastor.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah. I actually liked you guys.
jokes.setup: Monica and Chandler confess their lies and ask Erica to consider them anyway
jokes.punchline: Erica admits she genuinely liked them, making the deception more painful
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony that makes their lie more poignant - she liked the real them

dialogue.speaker: Erica
dialogue.line: That's what makes it worse. I actually liked you guys. I mean, I really liked you.

jokes.text: And don't tell her I said this, but the woman's always right.
jokes.setup: Chandler praising Monica's qualities to win over Erica
jokes.punchline: Admits universal truth about wives while Monica isn't listening
jokes.explanation: Classic relationship humor that rings true while serving the emotional moment

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: She's smart, she's funny, she's caring, she's beautiful...

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: The key to a happy marriage is a wife who doesn't listen to a word you say.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Nothing! You're great!

jokes.text: God bless you, Chandler Bing!
jokes.setup: Erica decides to give them the baby after Chandler's heartfelt speech
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top religious blessing using his full name
jokes.explanation: Decent emotional payoff with slight comedy in the formal blessing format

dialogue.speaker: Erica
dialogue.line: You know what? I've changed my mind. You two are gonna be great parents.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: Erica
dialogue.line: Yeah. I want you to have the baby.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh my God. Thank you so much. I promise you won't regret this. We're gonna love this baby so much, and we're gonna tell her all about you, and how brave you were.

dialogue.speaker: Erica
dialogue.line: I bless this baby with the power vested in me by the state of New York. In the name of the father, the son, and the holy ghost—Chandler Muriel Bing.

jokes.text: Turns out this sweater is made for a woman.
jokes.setup: Ross returns wearing the expensive sweater from shopping trip
jokes.punchline: Explains why it looked good - it's women's clothing
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that explains the earlier fashion success with logical twist

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Okay, okay, okay. I know what you're all thinking. But this sweater is perfect. It fits great, it's soft, it's comfortable...

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Ross, that's a women's sweater.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: No it's not!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yes it is. Look at the tag.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, that explains why it looked so good on me.

jokes.text: So why are you still wearing it?
jokes.setup: Ross admits the sweater is made for women
jokes.punchline: Chandler asks the obvious follow-up question
jokes.explanation: Simple setup for Ross's punchline, does its job efficiently

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: So... you're wearing a woman's sweater?

jokes.text: Because it's soft.
jokes.setup: Asked why he's still wearing women's sweater
jokes.punchline: Prioritizes comfort over masculinity with simple honesty
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Ross's unexpected pragmatism about comfort over image

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I don't know, it's comfortable!

jokes.text: Joey doesn't share food!
jokes.setup: Ross suggests they could do worse than spending evening together as friends
jokes.punchline: Joey's proud restatement of his catchphrase as episode ends
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback ending - reinforces the iconic catchphrase that defined the episode

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: How you doin'?

tmdb_overview: Nervous and excited, Monica and Chandler travel to Ohio to meet a young pregnant woman, Erica Simmons, who is considering allowing them to adopt her child when it's born. Upon meeting her they discover that due to a mix-up at the adoption agency, Erica thinks that Monica is a reverend and Chandler is a doctor. Meanwhile, Joey dates Phoebe's friend but annoys her with his territorial eating habits. Also Rachel picks out an outfit for Ross to wear on a date but due to a mix up he ends up in an awkward position.
insight: Low-density finale setup sacrifices laughs for emotional stakes, hitting just 2.75 jokes per minute.
